skill.name: project-domain
skill.description: Documents project domain vocabulary, entity relationships, naming rules, model locations, and validation rules for consistent AI-assisted development.
skill.summary: Create a shared domain glossary and relationship map for project-aware AI development.
skill.author: Consiliency

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and appear to be false positives from Markdown tables, placeholders, inline paths, and fenced examples. No executable code, network access, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at Markdown fence and inline-code locations. These lines are documentation formatting and integration path examples, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched content is inside a fenced Markdown example and an inline path reference. No command interpreter, script block, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Placeholder Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts match descriptive metadata, a table header, and a placeholder description line. No cryptographic function, library call, key handling, or hashing operation appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose or placeholder labels only. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern in Documentation Tables
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts match table rows for terminology and status fields. They describe domain modeling concepts and do not request host, network, account, or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are Markdown table headers. No commands, file enumeration, environment inspection, or network reconnaissance instructions are present.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when AI tools use inconsistent names for the same domain concepts. This skill creates a reusable glossary, relationship map, and validation guide for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Defines canonical names for domain entities and related concepts.
Records relationships between entities, including ownership and many-to-many links.
Lists model locations across Python, TypeScript, BAML, API schemas, and frontend types.
Captures status fields, valid transitions, and business validation rules.
Flags inconsistent terminology before AI-assisted changes introduce duplicate concepts.
limitations: The skill is a template and must be filled with project-specific entities.
It does not inspect source files or generate the glossary automatically.
It does not enforce rules at runtime or replace application validation.
Accuracy depends on keeping the document updated as the domain changes.

use_cases.title: Standardize Feature Planning
use_cases.description: Use the glossary before writing new user stories, models, or API endpoints.

use_cases.title: Improve AI Code Reviews
use_cases.description: Ask the assistant to compare proposed changes against accepted entity names and relationships.

use_cases.title: Onboard New Contributors
use_cases.description: Provide a compact domain map that explains core entities, locations, and business rules.

prompt_templates.title: Check Term Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change against the project-domain glossary. Identify any inconsistent terms, duplicate concepts, or unclear entity names.

prompt_templates.title: Add a New Entity
prompt_templates.scenario: Documenting a new model or API resource
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add a new domain entity to the project-domain skill. Include its canonical term, relationships, model locations, and validation rules.

prompt_templates.title: Trace Entity Relationships
prompt_templates.scenario: Understanding impact before a schema change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the project-domain relationships to trace the impact of changing this entity. Include related models, API schemas, frontend types, and status rules.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Domain Drift
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced architecture review across a codebase
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare this feature design with the project-domain vocabulary. Report terminology drift, missing relationships, invalid transitions, and rule conflicts.

output_examples.input: Review a proposed AccountProfile model against the glossary.
output_examples.output: The term may duplicate UserProfile. Use the existing canonical term unless AccountProfile has distinct ownership, lifecycle, and validation rules.

output_examples.input: Explain the relationship impact of adding SubscriptionPlan.
output_examples.output: Add SubscriptionPlan as a core entity, link it to Account and BillingRecord, then document valid status transitions and schema locations.

output_examples.input: Check whether the term customer can replace account.
output_examples.output: Do not replace account without a glossary update. Customer should be defined as a role, alias, or separate entity before use.

best_practices: Fill the glossary with real entities before using the skill for code generation.
Update relationship and status rules in the same change as domain model updates.
Use canonical terms in prompts, commit messages, API names, and documentation.
anti_patterns: Using placeholder glossary entries in production project guidance.
Adding synonyms without defining whether they are aliases, roles, or separate entities.
Treating the skill as a runtime validator instead of documentation guidance.

faq.question: Does this skill analyze my codebase automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured template for domain knowledge that users must complete and maintain.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I add first?
faq.answer: Start with core entities, canonical names, relationship rules, and model locations.

faq.question: Does it prevent invalid database states?
faq.answer: No. It documents expected validation rules but does not enforce application behavior.

faq.question: How often should the glossary change?
faq.answer: Update it whenever domain terms, entity relationships, statuses, or validation rules change.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains documentation only and no executable commands or malicious instructions.
